title: atraphaxis spinosa var. spinosa
intro: Atraphaxis spinosa var. spinosa, also known as the Devil's Shoestring, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of alkaloids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Atraphaxis spinosa var. spinosa include its potential to treat a variety of ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a diuretic and expectorant. Herbal preparations of Atraphaxis spinosa var. spinosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the roots or leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
